data.title: RTX's Raytheon wins $1.7 billion contract to deliver four Patriot fire units to Spain
data.description:
• Raytheon, a business of RTX Corp. (RTX), has been awarded a $1.7 billion contract to provide Spain with four Patriot air and missile defense systems, marking the country’s largest-ever order for the system, the company said Tuesday.
  • The foreign military sales contract includes radars, launchers, command and control stations, and training equipment.
  • The contract comes as Germany, the Netherlands, and Romania have placed orders for additional Patriot systems in 2025.

data.title: Cassava Sciences to pay $31.25M to settle securities class action suit
data.description:
• Cassava Sciences (SAVA) announced on Tuesday that it agreed to pay $31.25M to settle a class action lawsuit pending in a Texas District Court over the company’s alleged violations of the federal securities laws.
  • The case filed in the U.S. District Court for the Western District of Texas, Austin Division, in 2021 relied on allegations contained in Citizen Petitions submitted to the FDA regarding Cassava’s (SAVA) lead asset, simufilam.
  • In November 2024, the company suffered a late-stage trial setback for the once high-flying oral therapy targeted at Alzheimer's disease.
  • “The settlement is not an admission of fault or wrongdoing by the Company,” Cassava (SAVA) said, adding that it has recorded a $31.25M provision in Q2 2025 to recognize a potential loss contingency related to the case.

data.title: Canada’s monthly GDP falls 0.3% in October after September gain
data.description: • The monthly GDP MoM in Canada decreased to -0.30 percent in October from 0.20 percent in September of 2025.

data.title: Kroger approves additional $2B share buyback authorization
data.description:
• Kroger’s (KR) board approved an incremental $2B share repurchase authorization, supplementing the $7.5B program announced in December 2024.
  • Following the new approval, Kroger has ~$2.9B remaining under its total share repurchase authorizations as of December 23, 2025.
  • Share repurchases are expected to be funded through operating cash flow and existing liquidity, while maintaining Kroger’s investment-grade credit rating.

data.title: U.S. Gold raises ~$31.2M in private placement; shares down
data.description:
• U.S. Gold (USAU) closed a private placement issuing 1.92M common shares at $16.25/share and warrants for 961,077 shares, generating ~$31.2M in gross proceeds.
  • The warrants are immediately exercisable at $23/share and expire two years from issuance.
  • The offering price represented an ~4% discount to the December 15, 2025 closing price of $16.91.
  • New institutional shareholders include Franklin Templeton Investments, Mackenzie Investments, and Libra Advisors.
  • Proceeds will be used for initial development of the CK Gold Project, potential land acquisitions, exploration activities, and general working capital.
  • USAU shares down 8% premarket on Tuesday.

data.title: Mortgage delinquencies rise in November, in line with historical data, ICE Mortgage says
data.description:
The national mortgage delinquency rate rose to 3.85% in November, the highest level in more than four years, ICE Mortgage Technology, a part of Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), said on Tuesday in its First Look at Mortgage Performance for the month.

"While the topline delinquency numbers show a sharp increase, we’ve seen comparable spikes in prior years when November ended on a Sunday and scheduled payments didn’t post until early December," said Andy Walden, Head of Mortgage and Housing Market Research at ICE. "Overall performance was in line with what historical patterns would suggest."

Newly delinquent borrowers — those who were current in October but became delinquent in November — stood at 609,000, the largest single-month increase since May 2020, the ICE data said. Rolls from 30- to 60- to 90-day delinquency bands also rose sharply.

Foreclosure starts in November totaled 26,000, down 32% M/M due to seasonal and calendar effects, and up 25% Y/Y. Total U.S. foreclosure sales of 6,700 dipped 14% M/M and increased 25% Y/Y.

The monthly prepayment rate of 0.83% fell 18% M/M and rose 31% Y/Y.

 

iShares Mortgage Real Estate Capped ETF (REM) has dropped 1.6% in the past week, but has increased 5.0% in the year-to-date, still lagging the S&P 500's 17% climb. Meanwhile, the VanEck Mortgage REIT Income ETF (MORT) increased 2.1% in the past week and 3.3% YTD.

data.title: Invivyd granted FDA fast track status for COVID therapy
data.description:
Shares of microcap biotech Invivyd (IVVD) moved higher in the premarket on Tuesday after the Waltham, Massachusetts-headquartered company announced the FDA’s Fast Track designation for its experimental COVID therapy VYD2311.

The regulator has awarded the Fast Track designation for the use of VYD2311 for the prevention of COVID in individuals with underlying risk factors for severe forms of the disease, Invivyd (IVVD) said.

Designed as an alternative to COVID shots, the monoclonal antibody is currently undergoing late-stage development in the company’s Phase 3 DECLARATION clinical trial, which is expected to generate topline data in mid-2026.

Fast-track designation allows a quicker timeline for the development and review of drugs for serious conditions with unmet medical needs. Subject to certain conditions, companies with fast-track designations can secure the FDA's accelerated approval and priority review, paving the way for a swifter market launch of their products.

data.title: Trinity Biotech rises on order for 9M TrinScreen HIV tests
data.description:
• Trinity Biotech (TRIB) said on Tuesday that it has received an order for 9M units of its flagship HIV screening product, TrinScreen HIV. 
  • In addition, the company provided a trading update for its most recent financial quarters.
  • Trinity Biotech will produce the tests through its recently WHO-approved outsourced manufacturing process, which allows for efficient and cost-effective scalability. 
  • The company anticipates fulfilling the order during the fourth quarter of 2025 and the first quarter of 2026, as it fine-tunes its manufacturing and supply chain to accommodate this significant rise in demand.
  • This order is expected to positively impact Trinity Biotech’s revenue and profitability outlook. 
  • Trading Update
  • Revenues for Q3 2025 increased by 32% to $14.3m compared to $10.8m in Q2 2025. 
  • Gross profit for Q3 2025 was $6.5m compared to $4.6m in Q2 2025. 
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $0.5 million for Q3 2025 compared to negative $2.1m for Q2 2025.
  • Trinity Biotech (TRIB) shares were up 50% in pre-market trading.

data.title: BeMetals settles all outstanding debt
data.description:
• BeMetals (BMET:CA) said on Tuesday that it has reached an agreement to settle C$2.02M in outstanding debt held by B2Gold through the issuance of 28.9M common shares of the company at a price of C$0.07 per common share. 
  • In connection with the debt settlement, B2Gold has agreed to forgive the remaining US$4.41M of debt and accrued interest thereon owed to B2Gold to allow for the strengthening of BeMetals' balance sheet and enhance its financial flexibility for future growth initiatives. 
  • The company further announced that B2Gold is converting its C$5.3M of convertible debentures at C$0.25 per common share, in accordance with the terms of the convertible debentures, into 21.2M common shares and is converting C$838,208 of accrued interest on the convertible debentures at C$0.25 per common share.

data.title: Baiya International Group announces 1-for-25 reverse split record date
data.description:
• Baiya International Group (BIYA) has approved a reverse stock split of the company’s class A ordinary shares, a par value of $0.0001 each at a ratio of 1-for-25, with a post-reverse stock split par value of $0.0025.
  • The reverse stock split will be effective at 04:01 p.m. (ET) on Monday, December 29, 2025 and the ordinary shares will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis when the Nasdaq Stock Market opens for trading on Tuesday, December 30, 2025.
  • The company is undertaking the reverse stock split with the objective of meeting the minimum $1.00 per ordinary share bid requirement for maintaining the listing of the ordinary shares on Nasdaq.
  • Source: Press Release
